ogTitle: Hornsby Heights vs Horseshoe Bend: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hornsby Heights and Horseshoe Bend.
heading: Hornsby Heights vs Horseshoe Bend — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hornsby Heights, New South Wales and Horseshoe Bend, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Horseshoe Bend has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Hornsby Heights (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hornsby Heights or Horseshoe Bend safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hornsby Heights has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Horseshoe Bend scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hornsby Heights scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Horseshoe Bend scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hornsby Heights or Horseshoe Bend?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Horseshoe Bend has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
